name: Cream of Asparagus Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 sprig fresh thyme or 1 teaspoon dried thyme
Salt and freshly ground white pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the asparagus by soaking and rinsing them. Remove the top 1 inch of each spear and discard the tough lower part, keeping only the middle portion of the stalks.
2. In a 3-quart saucepan over medium heat, sauté the onion in butter for 3 to 5 minutes or until translucent.
3. Add the chicken stock, asparagus tips and stems, potato, celery, thyme, and pepper. Cover and bring to a boil.
4. Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 12 to 15 minutes or until the vegetables are soft. Remove from heat and let cool.
5. Blend half of the soup at a time in a blender container. Cover and blend at high speed for 20 to 30 seconds or until very smooth. Repeat with the remaining half.
6. Pass the blended soup through a fine sieve and return it to the saucepan. Add the reserved asparagus tips and cream (or half and half, or milk) and heat thoroughly. Alternatively, refrigerate and serve cold. Serves 4
